verbGlossary.PRICED FROM: {source} line item derives its unit cost or lump-sum value from {target}-based cost estimate (e.g., budget line priced from 5D cost model quantities)
verbGlossary.RECONCILED AGAINST: {source} is compared to {target}'s current cost baseline to validate scope and cost alignment
verbGlossary.PROJECTED FROM: Forecast or estimate at completion is calculated using {target}-linked cost and quantity data as the projection basis
verbGlossary.POSTED TO: {source}cost transaction is recorded against {target} to maintain real-time cost visibility
verbGlossary.ESTIMATED FROM: {source} or cost figure is derived from {target} quantity takeoff measurements (e.g., concrete volume, steel tonnage)
verbGlossary.SCOPED BY: {source} scope is defined using quantity takeoff values extracted from {target}
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIED BY: {source} scope is measured using updated {target} quantities to validate the change amount
verbGlossary.DERIVED FROM: {source} breakdown is generated from {target}-based quantity takeoff line items
verbGlossary.TRIGGERED BY: {source} is initiated because a coordination issue identified in {target} requires scope or cost adjustment
verbGlossary.ATTRIBUTED TO: {source} cost is traced back to a specific {target} as its root cause
verbGlossary.JUSTIFIED BY: {source} is supported by {target} that documents why additional funds are required
verbGlossary.ORIGINATED FROM: {source} traces its origin to a clash detected during {target} coordination review
verbGlossary.REDUCED BY: Cost growth metric improves as clash resolution rate increases, reducing rework-driven cost overruns
verbGlossary.LINKED TO: {source} is associated with a specific {target} element for element-level cost tracking
verbGlossary.MAPPED TO ELEMENT: {source} is assigned to {target} element so cost breakdowns align with the building information model structure
verbGlossary.CHARGED AGAINST: {source} expense is allocated to a specific {target} element for granular cost attribution
verbGlossary.VERIFIED AGAINST: Owner pay application quantities and values are validated by comparing to the as-built {target} installed conditions
verbGlossary.RELEASED UPON: {source} is released when the as-built {target} confirms the work is complete and documented
verbGlossary.SYNCHRONIZED WITH: {source} is time-phased using {target} construction sequence and schedule
verbGlossary.PHASED BY: {source} is calculated by phase using {target} schedule-linked work breakdown
verbGlossary.CORRELATED WITH: {source} rate is analyzed relative to {target} density to identify design quality impacts on cost
verbGlossary.DRIVEN BY: {source} burn rate increases as drawing revision frequency rises, consuming budget reserves
verbGlossary.IMPROVED BY: {source} variance performance improves with higher BIM coordination adoption, reducing unforeseen changes
